description: Project-neutral, multi-language holdouts for evidence-first code reasoning.

cases.task: Audit whether an accepted profile update is persisted.
cases.goodAnswerFixture:
ClaimType: wiring
Verdict: Bug (P0)
Claim: The accepted update is never persisted; build_update only constructs a value and ProfileService.update never calls save or database.write.
Evidence: project_source app/routes.py, app/service.py, app/repository.py.
BehaviorPath: entry(stageStatus=present) update_profile -> decision(stageStatus=present) validate/service.update -> side_effect(stageStatus=expected_missing) repository.save -> observer(stageStatus=present) accepted response.
Counterevidence: ProfileRepository.save contains database.write, but no supplied caller reaches it.
ProofLevel: SourceVerified
Unknowns: Dependency decorators or production middleware were not supplied.
cases.badAnswerFixture: The repository is constructed, so the update is fully persisted.

cases.task: Audit whether the declared retry setting controls runtime retry behavior.

snippets.content:
export type WorkerConfig = { retryLimit: number };
export const config: WorkerConfig = { retryLimit: 8 };

import { runWithRetry } from './retry';
export const processJob = (job: () => Promise<void>) => runWithRetry(job);

cases.goodAnswerFixture:
ClaimType: data_flow
Verdict: Bug (P1)
Claim: retryLimit is unused and does not reach the runtime loop; runWithRetry hard-codes attempt < 3.
Evidence: project_source src/config.ts and src/retry.ts.
BehaviorPath: entry(stageStatus=present) processJob -> dispatch(stageStatus=expected_missing) retryLimit handoff -> observer(stageStatus=present) loop bound attempt < 3.
Counterevidence: config.ts declares retryLimit = 8, but neither worker.ts nor retry.ts imports or reads config.
ProofLevel: SourceVerified
Unknowns: Generated wrappers and runtime injection were not supplied.
cases.badAnswerFixture: The retryLimit declaration proves it controls runtime retries.

cases.task: Audit whether the breaker can recover after opening.
cases.goodAnswerFixture:
ClaimType: state_transition
Verdict: Bug (P0)
Claim: After OnFailure opens the breaker, OnSuccess increments successes but never resets state to Closed, so it does not recover.
Evidence: project_source breaker/breaker.go.
BehaviorPath: entry(stageStatus=present) OnFailure -> decision(stageStatus=present) OnSuccess checks Open -> mutation(stageStatus=expected_missing) reset to Closed -> observer(stageStatus=present) Allow remains false.
Counterevidence: Closed exists and Allow reads it, but no supplied transition writes Closed after initialization.
ProofLevel: SourceVerified
Unknowns: Other files and concurrent access policy were not supplied.
cases.badAnswerFixture: OnSuccess sets the state to Closed, so the breaker can recover.

cases.task: Audit the claim that calling the supplied base publisher persists an order.
cases.goodAnswerFixture:
ClaimType: framework_semantics
Verdict: Bug (P1)
Claim: BasePublisher.publish only notifies listeners and does not persist, save, or insert the order.
Evidence: framework_source framework/BasePublisher.java and project_source orders/OrderService.java.
BehaviorPath: entry(stageStatus=present) OrderService.submit -> dispatch(stageStatus=present) BasePublisher.publish -> side_effect(stageStatus=present) listener.onEvent only.
Counterevidence: OrderRepository.save calls database.insert, but the supplied submit path never references OrderRepository.save.
ProofLevel: SourceVerified
Unknowns: Listener implementations and dependency wiring were not supplied.
cases.badAnswerFixture: BasePublisher persists the order because publish calls save internally.
